missionary.bio: Hi! I'm Michael Gledhill — Elder Gledhill out here. I grew up in American Fork, Utah as the oldest of five kids in a family that loves the gospel, hiking, and way too much football. I've been a member of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints my whole life, but it was during my senior year of high school that I gained my own testimony — through prayer, scripture study, and watching the gospel transform people around me.
missionary.testimony: I know that God lives and that He knows each of us by name. I know that Jesus Christ is our Savior — not just a historical figure, but a living, present, active force in our lives when we invite Him in. I know that the Book of Mormon is true scripture, and that reading it with real intent will change you. And I know — more than I know almost anything — that this work matters. These 24 months are the best thing I have ever done.

scripture.text: And the Spirit shall be given unto you by the prayer of faith.

timeline.event: Entered the MTC in Provo, Utah
Arrived in Idaho — First area: Rexburg

posts.title: A Family That Changed Everything
posts.body:
Dear family and friends,

This has been one of the most spiritual weeks of my entire mission. We've been teaching the Sorenson family for almost three months now, and this week something shifted. We were in their living room teaching about the Plan of Salvation, and you could feel the Spirit so strongly. The dad, who had been the most skeptical, started asking questions I've never heard an investigator ask. His heart is opening.

We also had a fantastic ward mission council meeting where we coordinated three member-present lessons. Members make ALL the difference — investigators trust them in a way they can't quite trust us yet as missionaries.

The weather has finally warmed up here. We biked 18 miles this week! My legs are thanking me... kind of. 😄 I love you all and feel your prayers every single day.

– Elder Gledhill
posts.scripture: And the Spirit shall be given unto you by the prayer of faith.

posts.title: Transfer Calls — I'm Staying!
posts.body:
Dear family,

Transfer calls came in on Saturday night and — I'm staying in Idaho Falls West! I honestly felt relief more than anything. We have so many promising investigators right now that I would have hated to leave. Elder Christensen is also staying, which means we get another full transfer together. He's one of the best companions I've had.

St. Patrick's Day was fun — half the ward showed up to church in green. One of our investigators, Sister Davis, attended sacrament meeting for the first time. She told us afterward she felt a peace she couldn't explain. Moments like that are why I'm here.

Thank you for all the letters this week — they seriously mean the world to me!

Love, Elder Gledhill
posts.scripture: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.

posts.title: A Baptism I'll Never Forget
posts.body:
Dear family,

We got to attend the baptism of Marcus Thompson this week — someone Elder Petersen and I started teaching back in September. I didn't think I'd ever get to see it, and then there I was, standing at the font with tears in my eyes.

Marcus stood up and bore his testimony right after and said he'd been searching for peace his whole life and finally found it. I could barely hold it together. The gospel changes lives — I know that more firmly every single week.

We also had our zone conference with President and Sister Harmon. He challenged each of us to pick a Christlike attribute to focus on. I chose patience.

With love, Elder Gledhill
posts.scripture: …how great shall be your joy with him in the kingdom of my Father!

posts.title: Snow, Service, and the Spirit
posts.body:
Dear family,

Idaho in March — sometimes it's 50 degrees, sometimes you get 8 inches of snow overnight. This week was the latter. We spent Monday shoveling driveways for elderly members in our ward, and every single house we finished led to a conversation about the gospel. Service really does open doors that knocking never could.

We had a powerful companionship study this week about the attributes of Christ. Elder Christensen and I have started role-playing lessons in the morning and it's made a huge difference in how confidently I teach.

Keep the letters coming — they are fuel for the soul.

— Elder Gledhill
posts.scripture: By their fruits ye shall know them.

messages.name: Grandma Ruth
messages.message: So proud of you Elder! We pray for you every morning.

messages.name: Tyler H.
messages.message: Miss you man! Keep crushing it out there. Go Cougars!

messages.name: Sister Johnson
messages.message: Your testimony has strengthened mine. Thank you.
